parts.name: Door Shelf Bin
parts.description: Clear plastic door shelf bin fits the left or right fresh food door. Compatible with many Whirlpool, Maytag, KitchenAid, and Amana side-by-side and French door refrigerators. Replaces cracked or broken door bins that no longer hold food securely.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator or turn off the circuit breaker before working on the door.
Locate the existing door bin on the refrigerator door liner — it sits in horizontal slots.
Grip the bin firmly with both hands, tilt the top edge outward slightly, then lift straight up to disengage from the retaining clips.
Align the new Door Shelf Bin (WPW10321304) with the door slots at the same height as the old bin.
Press down firmly until you feel both sides click into place. Give a gentle tug to confirm it is secured.
Restore power and reload the bin with food items.

parts.name: Ice Maker Assembly
parts.description: Complete ice maker assembly for Whirlpool and Maytag refrigerators. This unit includes the ice mold, ejector, motor, and thermostat as a pre-assembled kit. Replaces failed ice makers that produce no ice, make too little ice, or leave ice stuck in the mold.
install_steps: Turn off the ice maker using the on/off switch or arm, then unplug the refrigerator.
Remove the ice bin and set aside.
Use a Torx T15 or Phillips screwdriver to remove the 3 mounting screws holding the ice maker to the freezer wall.
Slide the ice maker forward to disconnect the wire harness plug — press the tab and pull straight out.
Connect the wire harness to the new Ice Maker Assembly (W10873791) until it clicks.
Position the new assembly against the freezer wall, aligning the mounting holes, and drive in the 3 screws snugly.
Replace the ice bin, turn the ice maker on, and plug in the refrigerator.
Allow 24 hours for the first ice production cycle.

parts.name: Water Inlet Valve
parts.description: Dual-outlet water inlet valve controls water flow to both the ice maker and the water dispenser. A failing valve can cause no water from the dispenser, no ice production, or slow water fill. This is a common repair for Whirlpool side-by-side and French door refrigerators.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator and shut off the water supply valve behind the unit.
Pull the refrigerator away from the wall to access the rear panel.
Use a Phillips screwdriver to remove the lower rear access panel (4–6 screws).
Note the tubing connections on the existing inlet valve, then use a wrench to loosen the water supply line fitting.
Squeeze the quick-connect tabs on the output tubes and pull them free from the valve.
Disconnect the two wire harness connectors from the solenoids.
Remove the valve mounting screw and pull the valve out.
Transfer the bracket to the new Water Inlet Valve (WPW10498990), mount it, and reverse the steps to reconnect tubes and harnesses.
Restore the water supply, plug in the refrigerator, and check for leaks.

parts.name: Ice Level Control Board
parts.description: The ice level control board uses an infrared sensor to detect when the ice bin is full and signals the ice maker to stop production. A faulty board can cause the ice maker to stop producing ice even when the bin is empty, or to overfill and jam.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator and remove the ice bin.
Locate the control board housing on the freezer interior wall, usually above the ice maker.
Press the release tab and tilt the housing cover forward to expose the board.
Disconnect the wire harness connector from the board.
Slide the old board out of its retaining tracks.
Slide the new Ice Level Control Board (W10190965) into the tracks until it clicks.
Reconnect the wire harness and close the housing cover.
Plug in the refrigerator, replace the ice bin, and verify normal ice production resumes.

parts.name: Upper Door Bin
parts.description: Clear plastic upper door bin for the refrigerator fresh food door. Mounts in the uppermost door slot and holds condiment jars, bottles, and small items. A direct replacement for cracked or missing upper door bins.
install_steps: Empty the bin of all items.
Tilt the top of the bin outward while lifting upward to release from the door slots.
Align the new Upper Door Bin (WPW10321302) with the top door slots.
Press down firmly until both sides snap into place.
Reload with items and confirm the bin sits level.

parts.name: Door Gasket
parts.description: Fresh food door gasket (seal) for Whirlpool refrigerators. A worn or torn gasket allows warm air to enter, causing the compressor to run continuously and frost to build up. This replacement restores a proper airtight seal.
install_steps: Open the refrigerator door fully and locate the gasket groove around the door perimeter.
Starting at a corner, pull the old gasket lip out of the retaining groove — it stretches and pulls free without tools.
Clean the groove channel with warm soapy water and dry thoroughly.
Soak the new Door Gasket (W11396033) in warm water for 15 minutes to make it pliable.
Starting at the top corner, press the gasket bead firmly into the groove, working around the door perimeter.
Close the door and check the seal by placing a dollar bill between the gasket and the cabinet — it should grip the bill with light resistance all the way around.
If any areas feel loose, press the gasket bead deeper into the groove with a flat-head screwdriver wrapped in tape.

parts.name: Crisper Drawer
parts.description: Full-width crisper drawer for the fresh food section. Designed with humidity-retaining channels to keep produce fresh longer. Replaces cracked or broken crisper drawers.
install_steps: Remove all produce from the existing crisper drawer.
Slide the drawer out along the support rails until it stops, then lift the front edge up and pull toward you to disengage from the rails.
Slide the new Crisper Drawer (W10321304) onto the rails, front edge first, and press down until it sits flat.
Push the drawer fully in to confirm smooth operation.

parts.name: Freezer Door Shelf
parts.description: Narrow freezer door shelf for storing ice cream bars, small frozen packages, and condiments. Fits the freezer door of Whirlpool side-by-side refrigerators. Replaces brittle or cracked shelves.
install_steps: Remove items from the existing freezer door shelf.
Lift the shelf up to clear the retaining tabs, then pull outward to remove.
Hook the new Freezer Door Shelf (WPW10594481) over the door tabs at the desired height.
Press down firmly until the shelf is secure.

parts.name: Gallon Door Bin
parts.description: Large gallon-size door bin designed to hold milk jugs, large juice containers, and 2-liter bottles. Fits in the lower fresh food door slots. Replaces cracked or broken gallon bins.
install_steps: Remove any items from the bin.
Grip the bin with both hands, tilt the top outward, and lift upward to release from the door slots.
Align the new Gallon Door Bin (WPW10321305) with the lower door slots.
Press down firmly until both sides click into place.

parts.name: Water Filter EveryDrop 1
parts.description: EveryDrop by Whirlpool refrigerator water filter 1 (EDR1RXD1). NSF certified to reduce 24 contaminants including lead and chlorine. Fits select Whirlpool, Maytag, Amana, and KitchenAid French door refrigerators. Replace every 6 months or 200 gallons.
install_steps: Locate the water filter in the upper right corner of the fresh food compartment.
Push the eject button to release the old filter, then pull it straight out.
Remove the protective cap from the new EveryDrop 1 filter.
Insert the new filter (W10295370) into the housing and push firmly until it clicks into place.
Run 4 gallons of water through the dispenser to flush the system before drinking.

parts.mfr_number: EDR3RXD1
parts.name: Water Filter EveryDrop 3
parts.description: EveryDrop by Whirlpool refrigerator water filter 3. Reduces chlorine taste, odor, and other contaminants. Fits select Whirlpool side-by-side refrigerators with the filter in the bottom left of the fresh food compartment. Replace every 6 months.
install_steps: Open the fresh food compartment and locate the filter at the base.
Turn the old filter counterclockwise (about a quarter turn) and pull straight out.
Remove the protective cap from the new EveryDrop 3 filter (EDR3RXD1).
Insert the new filter, align the tab, and turn clockwise until it locks.
Flush 4 gallons through the dispenser before use.

parts.name: Water Filter Housing
parts.description: Water filter housing that holds the refrigerator water filter in place. If the housing is cracked or the filter does not seat properly, water may bypass the filter or leak. Replaces the housing on select Whirlpool and Maytag French door refrigerators.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator.
Remove the water filter from the housing.
Unscrew the mounting screws holding the housing to the compartment ceiling.
Squeeze the tubing connector tabs and disconnect the water lines.
Mount the new Water Filter Housing (W10413645) in the same position and reconnect the water lines.
Install the water filter, restore power, and check for leaks.

parts.name: Ice Maker Water Fill Tube
parts.description: Plastic fill tube that channels water from the inlet valve into the ice maker mold. A cracked or misaligned fill tube causes water to drip in the wrong location, freezing into a block around the tube or leaking into the ice bin.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator and turn off the water supply.
Open the freezer and locate the fill tube leading into the ice maker.
Pull the fill tube straight out of its clip and off the inlet fitting.
Insert the new Ice Maker Water Fill Tube (WPW10341316) onto the inlet fitting and press it into the clip.
Restore power and water supply, then monitor for proper fill cycle.

parts.name: Defrost Thermostat
parts.description: The defrost thermostat monitors the evaporator temperature and cuts power to the defrost heater when the evaporator reaches the correct temperature. A failed thermostat can cause excessive frost build-up on the evaporator, blocking airflow and preventing proper cooling.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator.
Remove the freezer rear panel by unscrewing the mounting screws (usually 4–6).
Locate the defrost thermostat clipped to the evaporator coil.
Disconnect the two wire connectors from the thermostat terminals.
Unclip the old thermostat from the coil.
Clip the new Defrost Thermostat (WPW10388414) onto the evaporator at the same location.
Reconnect the wire connectors, replace the rear panel, and restore power.

parts.name: Evaporator Fan Motor
parts.description: Evaporator fan motor circulates cold air from the freezer evaporator coils throughout both the freezer and fresh food compartments. A failing motor causes warm temperatures, unusual noise, or no airflow. This is one of the most common refrigerator repairs.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator.
Remove all items from the freezer and take out the freezer shelves.
Remove the rear freezer panel (4–6 screws) to expose the evaporator assembly.
Locate the fan motor in the center of the evaporator — the fan blade pulls straight off the motor shaft.
Remove the motor mounting screws (usually 2) and disconnect the wire harness.
Mount the new Evaporator Fan Motor (WPW10189703) with the screws, reconnect the harness, and press the fan blade onto the shaft.
Replace the rear panel and freezer shelves, then restore power.
Verify the fan is running by listening for airflow with the door open momentarily.

parts.name: Condenser Fan Motor
parts.description: Condenser fan motor draws air over the condenser coils at the back of the refrigerator to dissipate heat. A failed condenser fan causes the compressor to overheat and the refrigerator to run warm. Usually located in the bottom rear of the unit.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator and pull it away from the wall.
Remove the rear lower access panel.
Locate the condenser fan motor next to the compressor.
Remove the fan blade from the motor shaft by pulling straight off.
Disconnect the wire harness and remove the motor mounting screws.
Install the new Condenser Fan Motor (WPW10131775), reconnect the harness, and push the fan blade onto the shaft.
Replace the access panel, restore power, and verify the fan spins when the compressor runs.

parts.name: Defrost Heater
parts.description: Electric defrost heater melts frost from the evaporator coils during the automatic defrost cycle. A burned-out heater leads to heavy frost accumulation on the evaporator, blocking airflow and causing poor cooling.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator and empty the freezer.
Remove the freezer rear panel to expose the evaporator coils.
Allow any remaining frost to melt naturally or carefully use a hair dryer on low.
Locate the heater element looping around or beneath the evaporator coils.
Disconnect the two wire connectors and slide the heater out of its brackets.
Insert the new Defrost Heater (WPW10715708) into the brackets and reconnect the connectors.
Replace the rear panel and restore power.

parts.name: Thermistor Temperature Sensor
parts.description: NTC thermistor that reports the fresh food or freezer compartment temperature to the electronic control board. A failed thermistor causes erratic temperature readings, resulting in the fridge running too cold or too warm.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator.
Locate the thermistor — typically clipped to an evaporator bracket or mounted on the compartment wall.
Disconnect the wire harness from the thermistor.
Unclip the old thermistor and discard.
Clip the new Thermistor (WPW10443051) into the same mounting location.
Reconnect the wire harness and restore power.
Set the temperature controls and verify the compartment reaches the set temperature within 4 hours.

parts.name: Compressor Start Relay
parts.description: Plug-in start relay that helps the compressor motor start. A failed relay causes a clicking sound every few minutes as the compressor tries and fails to start, and the refrigerator warms up. Shake the relay — if it rattles, it is almost certainly bad. This is the cheapest fix to try before replacing the compressor.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator and pull it from the wall.
Remove the lower rear access panel.
Locate the compressor — the relay plugs into the side of it.
Pull the relay straight out of the compressor — it may require a firm tug.
Shake the old relay: if it rattles, it has failed.
Plug the new Compressor Start Relay (WPW10135921) onto the compressor terminals.
Replace the access panel, push the refrigerator back, and restore power.
Listen for the compressor to start smoothly without clicking.

parts.name: Glass Shelf
parts.description: Full-width tempered glass shelf for the fresh food compartment. Includes the plastic trim frame. Replaces cracked, chipped, or broken shelves. Easily adjustable to multiple height positions on the interior side rails.
install_steps: Remove all items from the shelf and any shelf below it.
Tilt the front of the existing shelf upward and lift out of the rear supports, then pull forward.
Slide the new Glass Shelf (WPW10358498) in at the desired height — rear edge goes in first.
Lower the front edge onto the front rail notches.
Press down gently to confirm the shelf is level and secure.

parts.name: Deli Drawer
parts.description: Deli or snack drawer that fits beneath the full-width glass shelf in the fresh food compartment. Ideal for storing deli meats, cheese, and leftovers at a slightly colder temperature. Replaces cracked or broken drawers.
install_steps: Remove any items from the deli drawer.
Slide the drawer out fully, then lift the front edge and pull to disengage from the rails.
Slide the new Deli Drawer (W11168786) onto the rails and push in fully.
Test the drawer slides smoothly and does not catch.

parts.name: Pantry Drawer
parts.description: Wide pantry or produce drawer that spans the full width of the fresh food compartment bottom. Includes a humidity slider for controlling moisture levels. Replaces the main large crisper drawer.
install_steps: Remove all produce from the pantry drawer.
Slide the drawer fully out, lift the front, and pull away from the rails.
Transfer the humidity slider to the new Pantry Drawer (WPW10327249) if not included.
Slide the new drawer onto the rails and push in to confirm smooth operation.

parts.name: Humidity Control Slider
parts.description: Small plastic slider that controls the humidity vent in crisper and pantry drawers. Slide it open for low humidity (ideal for fruits) or closed for high humidity (ideal for vegetables). Replaces a broken or stuck slider.
install_steps: Locate the slider on the front face of the crisper or pantry drawer.
Press the retaining tabs and pull the old slider straight out.
Snap the new Humidity Control Slider (WPW10352139) into the slot until it clicks.
Test the slider moves smoothly between positions.

parts.name: Freezer Wire Shelf
parts.description: Chrome wire shelf for the freezer compartment. Hooks onto the freezer side rails and is adjustable to multiple heights. Replaces a bent, broken, or missing freezer shelf.
install_steps: Remove any frozen items from the shelf.
Tilt the shelf up and lift it off the rear rail hooks, then pull forward to clear the front hooks.
Hook the rear of the new Freezer Wire Shelf (W10869845) onto the rear rail notches at the desired height.
Lower the front edge onto the front rail notches and press down to seat.

parts.name: Door Switch
parts.description: Door switch (also called a door activated switch or light switch) that detects when the refrigerator door is open or closed. Controls the interior light and signals the control board to adjust fan settings. A failed switch can cause the light to stay on (warming the compartment) or never come on.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator.
Locate the door switch in the door frame, usually visible as a small button that the door presses when closed.
Use a flathead screwdriver to pop the switch out of its mounting hole.
Disconnect the wire harness from the old switch terminals.
Connect the harness to the new Door Switch (WPW10299665) and press it into the mounting hole until it clicks.
Restore power and test by opening and closing the door.

parts.name: LED Light Board
parts.description: LED light board that illuminates the fresh food compartment interior. Replaces incandescent bulb holders or failed LED assemblies. Direct fit for select Whirlpool and Maytag French door refrigerators.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator.
Locate the LED light assembly on the interior ceiling or side wall.
Remove the light cover (press tabs or unscrew).
Disconnect the wire harness from the LED board.
Remove the mounting screws and slide out the old board.
Slide the new LED Light Board (WPW10169733) in, fasten the screws, and reconnect the harness.
Replace the cover and restore power.

parts.name: Main Control Board
parts.description: Electronic main control board (PCB) that manages all refrigerator functions including temperature, defrost cycles, dispenser, and diagnostics. A faulty board can cause erratic temperatures, non-responsive controls, or the refrigerator not running at all.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator.
Access the control board — usually located in a housing at the top rear of the unit.
Photograph or label all wire harness connections before disconnecting.
Remove the mounting screws and unplug all harnesses from the old board.
Mount the new Main Control Board (WPW10130694) and reconnect all harnesses in the correct positions.
Restore power and run a diagnostic cycle per the owner manual.

parts.name: Temperature Control Thermostat
parts.description: Mechanical temperature control thermostat (also called a cold control) used in older Whirlpool and Maytag top-freezer and side-by-side models. Controls when the compressor runs based on compartment temperature. A stuck or failed thermostat can cause the refrigerator to run too cold, too warm, or not at all.
install_steps: Unplug the refrigerator.
Locate the thermostat dial and housing in the fresh food compartment ceiling or back wall.
Remove the housing cover screws and pull the cover off.
Disconnect the wire connectors and the sensing tube from the thermostat.
Unscrew the thermostat and remove it.
Install the new Temperature Control Thermostat (WPW10197428), route the sensing tube to the same location, and reconnect wires.
Replace the cover and restore power.

parts.name: Freezer Door Gasket
parts.description: Freezer door gasket (seal) for Whirlpool and Maytag side-by-side refrigerators. A damaged freezer door gasket allows warm air in, causing excessive frost, increased energy use, and food quality issues. This flexible magnetic seal restores an airtight closure.
install_steps: Open the freezer door fully.
Pull the old gasket lip out of the retaining groove starting at a corner.
Clean the groove thoroughly and dry it.
Soak the new Freezer Door Gasket (WPW10596822) in warm water for 15 minutes.
Press the bead into the groove starting at the top, working around the perimeter.
Test the seal with the dollar bill method around the entire door perimeter.
Adjust any loose sections by pressing deeper with a padded flat-head screwdriver.

parts.name: Fresh Food Door Gasket
parts.description: Fresh food compartment door gasket for Whirlpool side-by-side refrigerators. If food near the door is freezing, or you see moisture and frost on the gasket edge, the seal has failed. This replacement restores proper sealing.
install_steps: Open the fresh food door fully.
Starting at a corner, pull the old gasket bead out of the door liner groove.
Clean and dry the groove thoroughly.
Soak the new Fresh Food Door Gasket (WPW10321270) in warm water to restore flexibility.
Press the bead into the groove around the full perimeter.
Close the door and perform the dollar bill test all the way around.

parts.name: Drain Pump
parts.description: Drain pump assembly for Whirlpool and KitchenAid dishwashers. This pump removes water from the tub at the end of each wash and rinse cycle. A failed drain pump leaves standing water in the bottom of the dishwasher after a cycle. One of the most common dishwasher repairs.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher at the circuit breaker.
Turn off the water supply valve under the sink.
Remove the lower dish rack and unscrew the spray arm (counterclockwise).
Remove the filter assembly by twisting counterclockwise and lifting out.
Locate the drain pump at the bottom of the tub — it is a cylindrical unit secured with mounting tabs.
Rotate the pump counterclockwise to release it from its mount.
Disconnect the wire harness connector and the drain hose clamp.
Install the new Drain Pump (WPW10348269): connect the hose clamp, plug in the harness, and rotate clockwise to lock.
Replace the filter, spray arm, and dish rack.
Restore power and water supply. Run a short cycle and verify the pump drains.

parts.name: Circulation Pump and Motor
parts.description: Main circulation pump and motor assembly that drives water through the spray arms during wash and rinse cycles. A failing pump causes weak spray pressure, poor cleaning, or unusual grinding/humming noise. This is a larger repair that may require removing the dishwasher from the cabinet.
install_steps: Disconnect power at the circuit breaker and shut off the water supply.
Slide the dishwasher out from under the counter (disconnect the drain hose, water supply line, and power cord).
Lay the dishwasher on its back to access the underside.
Locate the circulation pump mounted to the bottom of the tub.
Disconnect all hoses, wire harnesses, and remove mounting screws.
Install the new Circulation Pump and Motor (WPW10482001), reconnect all connections, and torque mounting screws.
Return the dishwasher to position, reconnect all lines, and restore power.
Run a full wash cycle and check for leaks.

parts.name: Wash Pump Motor
parts.description: Wash pump motor for Whirlpool dishwashers. Drives water circulation during wash cycles. If the dishwasher fills with water but does not wash, or you hear a humming with no spray action, this motor may have failed.
install_steps: Disconnect power and water supply.
Remove the dishwasher from the cabinet for access to the bottom.
Disconnect the motor wiring harness and pump hoses.
Unscrew the pump motor mounting bolts and remove the old motor.
Install the new Wash Pump Motor (WPW10498900) and secure all connections.
Return the dishwasher to position and test.

parts.name: Lower Spray Arm
parts.description: Lower spray arm distributes water across the bottom rack during wash cycles. If spray holes are clogged or the arm is cracked, dishes on the bottom rack will come out dirty. Use a toothpick to clear clogged holes before replacing, but replace if the arm is physically damaged.
install_steps: Remove the lower dish rack from the dishwasher.
Unscrew the spray arm retaining nut counterclockwise (or pull straight up, depending on model).
Lift out the old spray arm.
Clear any debris from the water inlet port at the bottom of the tub.
Press the new Lower Spray Arm (WPW10491331) onto the inlet post and secure the retaining nut clockwise.
Reinstall the lower rack and run a wash cycle to confirm even water distribution.

parts.name: Upper Spray Arm
parts.description: Upper spray arm mounted beneath the upper dish rack. Sprays water upward to clean the bottom of the upper rack dishes. A cracked or clogged upper spray arm causes poor cleaning of glasses and plates in the top rack.
install_steps: Remove the upper dish rack from the dishwasher.
Locate the upper spray arm — it clips or screws to the water supply feed tube at the back of the tub.
Unscrew or unclip the old spray arm and pull it free.
Attach the new Upper Spray Arm (W10498900) to the feed tube and secure it.
Reinstall the upper rack and test with a wash cycle.

parts.name: Middle Spray Arm
parts.description: Middle spray arm (also called the tower spray arm) mounted between the upper and lower racks on three-rack dishwashers. Cleans the undersides of the upper rack and the top surfaces of the lower rack. Replace if cracked, warped, or spray holes are permanently blocked.
install_steps: Remove the middle rack if your dishwasher has one.
Unclip or unscrew the middle spray arm from its feed tube.
Attach the new Middle Spray Arm (WPW10350375) to the feed tube.
Replace the middle rack and verify the arm spins freely.

parts.name: Upper Rack Adjuster Kit
parts.description: Upper rack height adjuster kit for Whirlpool dishwashers. Allows the upper rack to be raised or lowered to accommodate tall items. A broken adjuster causes the upper rack to be stuck at one height or to drop unevenly during the wash cycle.
install_steps: Remove the upper dish rack from the dishwasher.
Locate the adjuster buttons on each side of the rack rails.
Pop the old adjuster assemblies off the rack side rails (they snap in place).
Snap the new Upper Rack Adjuster Kit (W10712395) components onto the side rails.
Reinstall the upper rack, test raising and lowering it, and confirm it locks securely at both heights.

parts.name: Lower Rack Wheel Assembly
parts.description: Lower dish rack wheel and axle assembly. The lower rack rides on 4–8 of these wheels on the tub rails. Broken or missing wheels cause the rack to drag, tip, or catch on the rails. Sold as a set of two wheels on one axle.
install_steps: Pull the lower rack fully out of the dishwasher and turn it over.
Locate the broken wheel assembly — it clips onto the rack corner wire.
Squeeze the retaining tabs and pull the old wheel assembly off.
Snap the new Lower Rack Wheel Assembly (W10195416) onto the rack wire corner until it clicks.
Repeat for any other broken wheels.
Reinstall the rack and test that it rolls smoothly on the rails.

parts.name: Upper Rack Slide Rail
parts.description: Upper rack slide rail that the upper dish rack rolls in and out on. If the upper rack falls off its track or catches during movement, the slide rail may be cracked or the detent tabs broken. Sold as a single rail — order two for a full set.
install_steps: Remove the upper rack from the dishwasher.
Unscrew the slide rail mounting screws from the inside tub wall.
Slide the old rail off its mounting bracket.
Mount the new Upper Rack Slide Rail (WPW10082853) and drive the mounting screws.
Reinstall the upper rack and test rolling it in and out.

parts.name: Cutlery Basket
parts.description: Plastic silverware and cutlery basket that hooks to the front of the lower dish rack. If the basket is cracked, missing its lid, or utensils are falling through broken dividers, this replacement restores full capacity.
install_steps: Remove the lower rack and any remaining cutlery.
Unhook the old basket from the rack front wire.
Hook the new Cutlery Basket (WPW10350376) onto the same position on the rack front wire.
Confirm it is secure before loading silverware.

parts.name: Door Balance Link Kit
parts.description: Door balance link (also called a door cable or strap) that connects the door spring to the door hinge, counterbalancing the door weight so it stays at any open angle. If the door falls open or slams shut, one or both balance links have snapped.
install_steps: Open the dishwasher door fully.
Locate the door balance link on each side of the door — it is a plastic strap or metal cable running from the spring to the door hinge area.
Unhook the old link from the spring and detach from the door hinge tab.
Hook the new Door Balance Link Kit (WP8194001) onto the spring and route it through the guide to the door hinge tab.
Repeat for the other side.
Test the door balance by opening to 90 degrees and releasing — the door should stay in place.

parts.name: Door Latch
parts.description: Door latch assembly for Whirlpool dishwashers. The latch engages a strike on the tub to keep the door closed during operation. If the dishwasher door does not click shut or the cycle does not start because the door sensor is not triggered, the latch has failed.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Open the door and locate the latch assembly at the top center of the door inner panel.
Remove the inner door panel screws and pull the panel away from the outer door.
Disconnect the wire harness from the latch switch.
Unscrew the latch mounting screws and remove the old latch.
Mount the new Door Latch (WPW10130004) and reconnect the wire harness.
Reassemble the door panel and restore power.
Test that the door clicks shut and a cycle can be started.

parts.name: Door Spring
parts.description: Door tension spring that works with the door balance link to hold the door at a chosen open angle. A broken spring causes the door to fall or not stay open. Typically located inside the toe panel or side cavity of the dishwasher.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Remove the lower toe kick panel to expose the spring mechanism.
Locate the spring attached to the door hinge and note its routing.
Use pliers to unhook the old spring from its retaining points.
Hook the new Door Spring (WPW10082854) onto the same retaining points.
Replace the toe kick panel, restore power, and test the door stays open.

parts.name: Filter Assembly
parts.description: Two-piece ultra-fine filter assembly (coarse filter + fine mesh filter) that catches food particles to prevent them from recirculating during wash cycles. A clogged filter is the number-one cause of poor cleaning and odors. Clean monthly; replace if the mesh is torn or the housing is cracked.
install_steps: Remove the lower dish rack.
Locate the filter in the bottom center of the dishwasher tub.
Turn the cylindrical upper filter counterclockwise about a quarter turn and lift it out.
Lift out the flat lower filter screen.
Rinse both parts under warm running water, using a soft brush to remove food residue.
If replacing: set the new flat filter screen in the recess, then insert the cylindrical filter and twist clockwise to lock.
Reinstall the lower rack.

parts.name: Coarse Filter Screen
parts.description: Flat coarse filter screen (lower filter) that sits beneath the cylindrical fine filter. Catches large food debris before it reaches the pump. Replace if torn or cracked. Often sold as part of the filter assembly kit.
install_steps: Remove the lower rack and lift out the cylindrical filter by turning counterclockwise.
Lift out the old coarse filter screen.
Drop the new Coarse Filter Screen (WPW10193994) flat into the filter recess.
Reinstall the cylindrical filter and turn clockwise to lock.

parts.name: Heating Element
parts.description: Electric heating element in the bottom of the dishwasher tub. Heats water during wash cycles and the air during dry cycles. A burned-out element leaves dishes wet after the drying phase and may also reduce wash water temperature.
install_steps: Disconnect power at the circuit breaker.
Remove the lower rack.
From inside the tub, note the element terminals at the bottom front.
From underneath the dishwasher (remove the toe kick panel), disconnect the wire connectors from the element terminals and remove the mounting nuts.
Pull the element out of the tub from inside.
Insert the new Heating Element (WPW10300981) through the tub holes, go underneath to thread on the mounting nuts, and reconnect the wire connectors.
Replace the toe kick and lower rack, then restore power.
Run a heated dry cycle and confirm dishes come out dry.

parts.name: High Limit Thermostat
parts.description: High limit thermostat is a thermal fuse-type safety device that cuts power to the heating element if the dishwasher overheats. Once tripped, it may not reset automatically. If your dishwasher is not heating water or not drying, test this thermostat for continuity before replacing the heating element.
install_steps: Disconnect power at the circuit breaker.
Remove the lower toe kick panel to access the underside of the tub.
Locate the thermostat — it is clipped or screwed to the tub bottom near the heating element.
Disconnect the two wire connectors.
Unclip the old thermostat and clip the new High Limit Thermostat (WPW10195091) in its place.
Reconnect the wire connectors, replace the toe kick, and restore power.

parts.name: Vent and Fan Assembly
parts.description: Vent and fan assembly that opens at the end of the wash cycle to exhaust moist air and aid drying. If the vent flap is stuck closed, the fan fails, or the actuator does not open the vent, dishes will remain wet after the dry cycle.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Open the dishwasher door and locate the vent — it is typically on the inner door panel near the top.
Remove the inner door panel screws to separate the inner and outer door panels.
Disconnect the wire harness from the fan motor.
Remove the retaining screws and pull out the old vent assembly.
Install the new Vent and Fan Assembly (WPW10082901), reconnect the harness, and reassemble the door panel.
Restore power and run a drying cycle to verify the vent opens.

parts.name: Detergent Dispenser
parts.description: Soap and rinse aid dispenser assembly for the dishwasher door inner panel. Includes the detergent cup with a spring-loaded flip lid and a rinse aid compartment. If the detergent cup does not open during the wash cycle, or the lid is broken, dishes won't clean properly.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Open the door and remove the inner door panel screws.
Separate the inner and outer door panels.
Disconnect the actuator wire harness from the dispenser.
Unscrew the dispenser mounting screws from the inner panel.
Pull out the old dispenser and transfer any rinse aid to the new unit.
Mount the new Detergent Dispenser (W10169313), connect the harness, and reassemble the door panel.
Restore power, add detergent, and run a wash cycle to confirm the cup opens at the right time.

parts.name: Rinse Aid Dispenser Cap
parts.description: Rinse aid compartment cap that seals the rinse aid reservoir. If the cap is cracked or does not close properly, rinse aid can leak during the wash cycle. A simple, low-cost fix.
install_steps: Open the dishwasher door and locate the rinse aid compartment cap next to the detergent cup.
Turn the old cap counterclockwise and pull it off.
Press the new Rinse Aid Dispenser Cap (WPW10080043) into the opening and turn clockwise to lock.
Fill with rinse aid and confirm the cap is sealed.

parts.name: Control Board
parts.description: Electronic control board (main PCB) that manages all dishwasher functions: cycle selection, water temperature, fill/drain timing, and diagnostics. Error codes, unresponsive controls, or the dishwasher failing to start can all point to a faulty control board.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Open the door and remove the inner door panel screws.
Separate the inner and outer door panels.
Photograph all wire harness connections on the control board.
Disconnect all harnesses and remove the board mounting screws.
Mount the new Control Board (WPW10296024) and reconnect all harnesses.
Reassemble the door panel, restore power, and run diagnostics per the owner manual.

parts.name: Door Control Panel
parts.description: User interface control panel (UI board) with the cycle selection buttons and display. A cracked panel, unresponsive buttons, or a blank display usually means the UI board has failed. Works alongside the main control board.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Open the door and remove the inner door panel.
Disconnect the ribbon cable or wire harness from the UI panel.
Unscrew or unclip the old panel from the door frame.
Attach the new Door Control Panel (WPW10300440) and reconnect the cable.
Reassemble the door and restore power.

parts.name: Water Inlet Valve
parts.description: Solenoid water inlet valve that opens to allow water to fill the dishwasher tub at the start of each cycle. A failed valve causes the dishwasher to not fill (E1 error), fill very slowly, or continue filling when it should stop. Located at the bottom of the dishwasher behind the access panel.
install_steps: Disconnect power and shut off the water supply under the sink.
Remove the lower toe kick panel.
Locate the water inlet valve at the left front corner of the dishwasher bottom.
Turn off the water, place a towel down, and disconnect the supply line with pliers.
Disconnect the internal water hose and the wire harness.
Remove the mounting screw and pull out the old valve.
Install the new Water Inlet Valve (WPW10327249), reconnect all hoses and the harness.
Restore the water supply and power, then run a short cycle and check for leaks.

parts.name: Float Switch
parts.description: Overfill float switch assembly that prevents the dishwasher from flooding. A plastic float in the tub bottom rises with water level; if it gets stuck in the raised position, the dishwasher will not fill. If stuck down, the tub may overfill. A common cause of E1 fill errors.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher.
Remove the lower rack and locate the float — it is a small dome or cylindrical plastic piece near the front left corner of the tub bottom.
Pull the float dome straight up to remove it.
From underneath the tub (remove the toe kick panel), disconnect the wire harness from the float switch and unscrew the switch.
Install the new Float Switch (WPW10254672) from underneath and reconnect the harness.
Press the float dome back onto the switch post.
Restore power and run a fill cycle.

parts.name: Fill Hose
parts.description: Internal fill hose that carries water from the inlet valve to the dishwasher tub. A cracked or leaking fill hose will drip water under the dishwasher onto the floor. Replace the hose if you see water pooling under the unit and have ruled out the door seal.
install_steps: Disconnect power and water supply.
Remove the toe kick panel.
Locate the fill hose running from the inlet valve to the tub.
Use pliers to slide the hose clamps back and pull the hose off both fittings.
Install the new Fill Hose (WPW10350377) onto both fittings and position the clamps over the connections.
Restore power and water, run a cycle, and verify no drips.

parts.name: Tub Cover
parts.description: Plastic cover that sits over the sump area at the bottom center of the dishwasher tub. Directs water flow to the circulation pump. If the cover is cracked or missing, water circulation will be uneven and cleaning will suffer.
install_steps: Remove the lower rack and spray arm.
Lift the tub cover straight up to remove it (no tools needed on most models).
Drop the new Tub Cover (WPW10300441) in place over the sump.
Press down to seat it and reinstall the spray arm and lower rack.

parts.name: Silverware Basket
parts.description: Full-size silverware basket with divided compartments for forks, knives, and spoons. Includes a removable lid. Hooks to the lower rack front. Replaces broken or missing silverware baskets.
install_steps: Remove the lower rack.
Hook the new Silverware Basket (WPW10080844) onto the lower rack front wire.
Confirm the basket is secure, load silverware, and replace the rack.

parts.name: Adjustable Middle Rack
parts.description: Adjustable middle (third level) rack for Whirlpool dishwashers with three-rack capacity. Slides in above the upper rack to hold spatulas, ladles, and shallow bowls. Replaces a bent, cracked, or missing third rack.
install_steps: Slide the adjustable rack into the upper guide rails at the top of the dishwasher interior.
Ensure the rack clicks into the end stops on both sides.
Load flat items on the rack and run a wash cycle to verify water reaches all levels.

parts.name: Drain Hose
parts.description: Corrugated drain hose that carries waste water from the drain pump to the sink drain or garbage disposal. A kinked, cracked, or blocked drain hose causes poor draining or standing water in the tub. Typically 6-7 feet long with clamp connectors on both ends.
install_steps: Disconnect power to the dishwasher at the circuit breaker.
Pull the dishwasher out from under the counter slightly to access the rear.
Locate the drain hose running from the drain pump at the bottom of the dishwasher up to the sink drain or disposal.
Use pliers to slide the hose clamps back, then pull the hose off both fittings.
Route the new Drain Hose (WPW10082905) along the same path, ensuring a high loop or air gap to prevent backflow.
Secure both ends with hose clamps and push the dishwasher back into position.
Restore power and run a drain cycle to confirm water exits properly.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Frigidaire for Frigidaire, Kenmore, Crosley, Westinghouse
parts.name: Door Shelf Retainer Bar 240534901
parts.description: This door shelf retaining bar, also known as refrigerator door bar retainer or fridge door rack, is a genuine part sourced from the original manufacturer. It is a white This manufacturer-certified Door Shelf Retainer Bar, sometimes otherwise known as the door rack or bar retainer,  is a white, plastic part which can be easily installed onto the inside of the refrigerator or freezer door to hold items on the door shelves.  It holds items in your fridge and will show physical damage if broken or warped, at which time it should be replaced. Check your user manual and model number to see if this part is right for you. No tools are required for this installation, the part should simply snap into place. This part measures roughly 25 inches wide, 3 inches high, and 5 inches deep. This part is sold individually, and we recommend checking the other door shelf retaining bars on your refrigerator or freezer at the same time as ordering this part to ensure that no others need to be replaced, as they often wear or warp at similar speeds.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Frigidaire for Frigidaire, Kenmore, Crosley, Westinghouse
parts.name: Freezer Door Shelf Retainer Bar 240534801
parts.description: This is the replacement door shelf retainer bar for your freezer door. This retainer part connects to the freezer door wall, and prevents items that are stored in the freezer door from falling out when the door opens and closes. The main reason you would want to replace the retainer bar is if the old bar is broken or missing. To replace the retainer bar, simply pop the old bar out of position and snap the new one into place. This retainer bar is approximately 24 inches long and 5 inches wide. It is made of white plastic and is sold individually.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for KitchenAid, Whirlpool, Maytag, Kenmore
parts.name: Lower Dishrack Wheel W10195416
parts.description: This OEM gray Dishwasher Lower Dishrack Wheel Assembly is a plastic kit that allows the rack to slide in and out of the dishwasher easily. If it breaks or becomes damaged. sliding will feel difficult. This can happen due to normal wear and tear, and the part should be replaced if this occurs. Check your model number and user manual to make sure this part is correct. It attaches directly to the dishrack. To repair, open the dishwasher door and pull out the lower dishrack. Next, push the tab from both sides and it should pop out. To install the new wheel assembly, place it on the rack and make sure the caps overlap the wire from the inside before you clip it in place by pushing the wheel. It measures roughly 3x6 inches.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Bosch for Bosch
parts.name: Dishwasher Handlecap Shaped 00628998
parts.description: This door handle end cap is supplied directly by the manufacturer for use with dishwashers. It is a genuine OEM part that is designed to fit just like the original piece. The end cap is made of highly durable plastic, and it is responsible for securing the end of the handle on the dishwasher door. It protects the handle from scratches, dents, and general wear and tear, which can occur over time. It also contributes to the appearance of the dishwasher by concealing any exposed screws and providing a finished look to the handles. When replacing the end caps, you will need to first remove the door panel. Each end cap is sold individually.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by GE for General Electric
parts.name: Refrigerator Water Filter RPWFE
parts.description: This NSF-certified water filter helps to improve the taste, quality, and health of your water by removing a number of contaminants including mercury, lead, and trace pharmaceuticals. The filter should be replaced every six month or after 300 gallons of water have been filtered. If you are unsure if your filter is due for replacement, another indicator is if the water flow from the dispenser has slowed down considerably. Replacing this part is a simple task that does not require any tools or shutting off the water or power supply to your fridge. Once the new filter has been installed, be sure to run at least two gallons of water through before consuming.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool, Kenmore, KitchenAid
parts.name: Upper Rack Adjuster Kit - White Wheels, Left and Right Sides W10712395
parts.description: This authentic Dishwasher Upper Rack Adjuster Kit with white wheels is a kit made up of primarily plastic and metal components, which will require a screwdriver to install. This kit comes with all the necessary parts for a dishwasher rack adjuster.  It fits onto the dishwasher rack and connects it to the track allowing it to slide in and out. If broken then the rack will no longer slide properly, and the part must be replaced. In many cases, our customers have reported the top rack of their dishwasher hanging down or sagging due to the adjuster kit, particularly if the wheels, have broken or failed. If you are having problems in this area, this kit will help get your dishwasher back up and running. Check your model number and user manual to see if this part is right for you. The parts for this kit come as a package and are not generally sold individually.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by LG for LG, Kenmore
parts.name: TRAY ASSEMBLY, DRAWER AJP73594506
parts.description: Introducing the LG TRAY ASSEMBLY, DRAWER. Crafted by LG, this genuine OEM item brings efficiency and organization to your refrigerator. As a freezer drawer bin, it offers a convenient solution for orderly food storage in your refrigerator's freezer. It's designed specifically to fit various models of LG fridges and freezers. Installation is simple and straightforward, with a recommended reference to your owner's manual for precise instructions. Enjoy a more organized freezer space with this quality LG drawer bin tray assembly.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Whirlpool, Maytag, KitchenAid, Kenmore
parts.name: Refrigerator Ice and Water Filter EDR1RXD1
parts.description: The EDR1RXD1 water filter provides clean, fresh-tasting water by reducing contaminants such as lead, chlorine, and particulates. Designed for easy installation, this filter ensures your refrigerator water and ice are safe and refreshing with every use

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Frigidaire for Frigidaire, Crosley, Kenmore, Westinghouse
parts.name: Dishwasher Door Bottom Seal 5304500204
parts.description:
This door seal is for dishwashers.

Door seal seals the gap between the opening to the dishwasher tub and the outer edge of the door.

After installing the door seal, shut the dishwasher door and leave the door closed for several hours to properly seat the new door seal.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Bosch for Bosch, Thermador
parts.name: PUMP-DRAIN 00631200
parts.description: This drain pump is a genuine OEM replacement part that is specially made for various models of dishwasher. It is responsible for removing water from the dishwasher at the end of a cycle. It pumps the water out through the drain hose, which is connected to the kitchen sink plumbing or a dedicated drain line. If you notice an unpleasant odor and water pooling at the bottom of your dishwasher after a cycle, it could indicate a problem with the drain pump. If the pump is faulty you may also hear unusual rattling and grinding sounds.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Maytag, KitchenAid, Whirlpool, Amana
parts.name: Refrigerator Water Filter EDR4RXD1
parts.description: This water filter (Whirlpool EveryDrop Refrigerator Water Filter, Single Turn Filter) purifies water going to the water dispenser and sometimes the icemaker as well. This part should be replaced if it is causing reduced water flow or just replaced periodically per the owner manual. Replacing this water filter may fix a noisy or leaking fridge, a fridge that is not dispensing water (or reduced flow rate), and an ice maker that is not making ice at all, or not making enough ice. This filter is white, sold individually and measures 8 inches by 3 inches in diameter. This water filter has been updated by the manufacturer. It may appear different but will still function the same. It is sold individually.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Bosch for Bosch, Thermador
parts.name: Dishwasher Circulation Pump with Heater 12008381
parts.description: This heat pump assembly is a vital component for select Bosch, Thermador, and Gaggenau dishwashers, combining circulation and heating functions to ensure effective cleaning performance. It pumps water through the spray arms while simultaneously heating it to the optimal temperature for thorough dishwashing. As a genuine OEM part, it is engineered for compatibility and long-term reliability. Replacing a faulty heat pump can resolve issues such as poor cleaning results, failure to start, water leaks, or unusual operational noises. Installation should be performed with care, and compatibility should be confirmed using your dishwasher’s model number to ensure proper fit and function.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool, KitchenAid, Amana
parts.name: Water Tube Kit W10823511
parts.description: This is the replacement water tube kit for your refrigerator. This tubing is made of plastic, is 1/4 inch in diameter, and comes with 2 inserts. This tube may be longer than the original tube, and may need to be cut. This water tube transports water to the ice and water dispensers. If your refrigerator is leaking, or if the ice and water dispensers are not dispensing properly, you may need to replace this tube. This part is sold individually and is an OEM part sourced directly from the manufacturer.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for KitchenAid
parts.name: Dishwasher Electronic Control Board W10854225
parts.description: The electronic control board manages the functions of the dishwasher such as washing, draining and drying.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for KitchenAid, Kenmore, Whirlpool, Maytag
parts.name: Seal W10864081
parts.description: This is a seal, but you may also know it as a door gasket and it is for your dishwasher. The seal forms a watertight barrier between the door and the dishwasher tub, deterring water from escaping during a wash cycle. If your dishwasher is leaking you will need to replace the seal, as it is likely damaged. The first step should be to check the seal for buildup of food particles near the sides and the bottom of your appliance.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool
parts.name: Electric Control Board W10854221
parts.description: Ensure your Whirlpool dishwasher operates at its peak with this genuine OEM Control-Elec Board. Designed to manage all vital functions of your dishwasher including washing, draining, and drying, it ensures seamless performance. Compatibility spans across multiple Whirlpool models, making it a versatile addition for your appliance repair needs. It's advised to unplug the dishwasher prior to installation and use work gloves for hand protection. Please verify that this control board is suitable for your model before ordering. Embrace high-quality control with this Whirlpool-exclusive product.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Whirlpool, Kenmore, Maytag, KitchenAid
parts.name: Refrigerator Ice Maker W10873791
parts.description: Tired of your fridge not making ice? This replacement ice maker can fix that. It automatically fills with water, freezes it, and drops ice into the bin—so you always have ice ready for drinks or cooking. It’s made to fit several Whirlpool, Maytag, and KitchenAid models and is great if your current ice maker is leaking, noisy, or just not working. Easy to install and ready to get your ice supply back on track.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool, Maytag, Amana
parts.name: Pantry End Cap Kit, LH and RH W10874836
parts.description: This manufacturer-approved Refrigerator Pantry End Cap Kit, also known as the Refrigerator Drawer Support, is a kit containing all parts of the Pantry End Cap.  It snaps into place on the appliance’s pantry drawer to help contain the items inside and to keep your refrigerator door from sliding all the way out. It is rated as "medium" difficulty to install, is constructed of plastic and comes in white. If broken or missing, the pantry drawer will not stay in place and the part should be replaced. Refer to your user manual and model number to ensure this is the correct part for you. This kit includes both the right and left side end caps, as well as the pins that will hold them in place. To replace an end cap, remove the three screws holding it in place. Then, remove the pin and end cap, replacing the old end cap with the new end cap. In the past, the left and right end caps were sold separately, but the part has been updated and now includes both sides. The part is sold as a kit, and unfortunately, as such none of the parts are available individually.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Whirlpool, Kenmore, Amana, KitchenAid
parts.name: Dishwasher Drain Pump W10876537
parts.description: If your dishwasher isn’t draining properly or leaving water behind, this drain pump motor assembly might be the fix you need. It helps clear water from the tub during the drain cycle, keeping your appliance running smoothly and your dishes clean. This genuine replacement part is made to fit a variety of models from Whirlpool and related brands like Kenmore, KitchenAid, and Amana, making installation straightforward and dependable.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Amana, KitchenAid, Maytag, Kenmore
parts.name: Refrigerator Water Filter Cover WP12568001
parts.description: This part is the replacement water filter cover for your refrigerator. It is made of white plastic and is approximately 13 inches long and 4 inches wide at the widest point. The water filter cover protects the water filter from damage while it is installed in the refrigerator. The main reason to replace the water filter cover is if it is cracked, damaged, or missing. This part is sold individually and is an OEM part sourced directly from the manufacturer.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Whirlpool, Inglis, Kenmore, Roper
parts.name: Refrigerator Door Shelf Bin WP2187197K
parts.description: Refrigerator Door Shelf Bin Specifications Sold Individually.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool, KitchenAid
parts.name: Refrigerator Water Inlet Valve 120v 50/60hz WP2188808
parts.description: This is a double solenoid valve.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Whirlpool, Kenmore, KitchenAid, Maytag
parts.name: Refrigerator Seal WP2198628
parts.description: This part is a replacement refrigerator drive shaft seal for your refrigerator. The drive shaft seal protects the auger motor from water and condensation that may leak down the drive shaft from the ice bucket. If the seal is broken, water and condensation can get in, causing the ice maker to fail. To replace the seal, first remove the ice bucket from your freezer. Then remove the two screws on the drive shaft holder, and use a pair of pliers to remove the drive shaft. At this point you can remove the seal from the drive shaft. Be sure to clean the area that the seal sits in before you reinstall the drive shaft. This part is white in color and is about an inch in diameter. The seal is sold individually.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Whirlpool, Kenmore, Maytag, Amana
parts.name: Door Shelf WP2309941
parts.description: This authentic Whirlpool refrigerator part is a door shelf rail. It snaps into your refrigerator door and prevents items from sliding or falling out when your open the door. It is made of plastic and is approximately 17 inches in length by 5 inches in width. To replace this part, simply snap out the old door shelf rail and install the new one.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool, Maytag, KitchenAid
parts.name: Refrigerator Single Water Inlet Valve WP2315576
parts.description: The single water inlet valve, also known as a water solenoid valve, is used for refrigerators. This part is a solenoid-operated switch that releases water from the supply line to the water valve and into the icemaker mold when activated. If your icemaker is making small cubes, does not dispense ice or water, or leaks, this replacement could solve the issue. The tools needed for this repair are a 1/4 inch nut driver and a utility knife. Refer to the diagram provided by the manufacturer for further instructions. As a safety precaution, remember to unplug your dryer from the power source.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for KitchenAid, Whirlpool, Insinkerator
parts.name: Dishwasher Water Inlet Valve WP4171000
parts.description: This valve replaces old two coil valves. The attaching solenoid on the valve open and close according to the desired amount of water needed.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool, Maytag, KitchenAid
parts.name: Washer Hose Clamp WP596669
parts.description: This hose clamp is used in a wide variety of household appliances. Most often it is found in washers, dishwashers, refrigerators, freezers, dehumidifiers, garbage disposals, or air conditioners. This clamp is used to securely attach a hose to another part of your appliance, which helps prevent leaks from those connections. Depending on the appliance, it can attach hoses to drain pumps, water inlet valves, pressure switches, and injector nozzles. We recommend you refer to model-specific diagrams for appropriate uses and exact placement. This hose clamp is made of metal, and is sold individually. To access and replace this part you will need a nut driver, pliers, and screwdrivers. Before you begin any repair work, make sure you have unplugged your appliance, and shut off the water supply if applicable.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Maytag, Jenn-Air
parts.name: Refrigerator Water Inlet Valve - 115V WP61005627
parts.description: This valve is to supply water to your refrigerator for the ice maker or the water dispenser.  It's also useful for filtering your water as it comes through the line.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Jenn-Air, Maytag
parts.name: Defrost Heater and Thermostat Assembly WP61006199
parts.description: This part assembly has 2 functions:  The defrost heater keeps the cooling coils from frosting over and the thermostat senses that the heat near the cooling coils has reached the desired temperature.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool, KitchenAid, Maytag
parts.name: Dishwasher High Limit Thermostat WP661566
parts.description: This part acts as as safety mechanism that keeps the dryer from overheating. Once the dryer temperature reaches a certain point, this part will shut down power to the heating element.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Whirlpool, KitchenAid, Kenmore, Maytag
parts.name: Dishwasher Mounting Bracket WP8269145
parts.description: The mounting bracket is sold individually, without screws. This part secures the upper side of the dishwasher to the underside of the counter or cabinet to prevent the dishwasher from moving, shaking, or tilting forward. The bracket is made entirely of metal. It is recommended to check all brackets, if applicable, because they often wear at the same rate and both may need to be replaced. This is an OEM part that is sourced from the authentic manufacturer. Tools needed are a pair of needle nose pliers and a screwdriver.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, Whirlpool, Inglis
parts.name: Dishwasher Water Inlet Valve WP8274220
parts.description: This dishwasher water inlet valve is a precision-engineered component responsible for regulating the flow of water into the appliance during each cleaning cycle. Located behind the lower kickplate panel, it uses solenoids to open and close, ensuring the correct amount of water enters the dishwasher. A malfunctioning valve may result in issues such as the dishwasher not filling, overfilling, or leaking. Designed to meet original equipment standards, this part offers reliable performance and compatibility with a range of models. Replacement is recommended when water flow problems occur, and installation should follow manufacturer guidelines for safety and accuracy.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Kenmore, KitchenAid, Whirlpool, Inglis
parts.name: Dishwasher Water Inlet Valve WP8531669
parts.description: This water inlet valve should be located behind the lower kickplate panel in either the right or left corner. The attaching solenoid on the valve will open and close according to the desired amount of water needed.

parts.name: Dishwasher Rack Track Stop WP8565925
parts.description: The rack track stop on your dishwasher is designed to stop the dish rack at the appropriate place when opening and closing the rack. If your dishwasher is not cleaning your dishes properly, the rack stop may need to be replaced. The replacement part may differ slightly from the broken part, depending on the model. However, the different styles are interchangeable. This product is sold individually and comes in both a white and grey plastic version. Many of our customers found this repair to be very easy and takes less than 30 minutes to complete. Simply snap the old part out and snap the new part into place. You will not require any tools to complete the job. This is an authentic part approved by the manufacturer.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for KitchenAid, Kenmore, Whirlpool
parts.name: Dishwasher Strike Plate WP8574157
parts.description: This part is mounted on the top of the dishwasher.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for Maytag, Amana, Magic Chef, Crosley
parts.name: Heating Element WP99002360
parts.description: This dishwasher heating element is not designed to heat the water that enters it, but is designed to maintain the heat of the water that enters the dishwasher. It is located on the bottom of the dishwasher.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for KitchenAid, Kenmore, Whirlpool, Kenmore
parts.name: Dishwasher Tine Pivot WPW10082853
parts.description: The tine pivot clip, also known as the tine row pivot, is used in dishwashers and is located in the upper dishrack. At around 3 inches in length, the clip is used to keep the tine row in place in the dishrack. If your clip is cracked or damaged, it is best to replace this part. To repair, first lift the release tab and hold the other side to prevent it from falling off. After lifting it off, you can set the new clip on the tine row and lock it up. Refer to the manual provided by the manufacturer for further installation instructions and guidance. Remember to wear work gloves as a safety precaution. This repair was rated as very easy by our customers and should be done in 15 minutes or less.

parts.mfr_number: Manufactured by Whirlpool for KitchenAid, Kenmore, Whirlpool, Maytag
parts.name: Dishwasher Heating Element WPW10082892

To begin this repair, locate the screws that hold the hinge to the refrigerator frame and remove them. Carefully lift the door off the hinge and set it aside. Remove the faulty hinge and position the new one in its place. Reattach the door to the new hinge and secure the hinge to the refrigerator frame using the appropriate fasteners.

If your dishes are not drying or the water in the dishwasher is not getting hot you may need to replace the element. This kit includes the heater, two heater fasteners, and is a genuine OEM part. Please remember to disconnect the power before starting the repair. Use a multi-meter to test your heating element for continuity to determine whether this is the part that is causing your symptom. This part does not include any washers. To make your repair, disconnect the wires from the bottom of the element in the back, remove the plastic nuts holding it in place, remove the element, and repeat these steps in reverse order to install your new part. This is an easy repair that requires only a screwdriver, wrench set, and should take less than 30 minutes.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: ice maker not working, no ice, ice maker stopped, ice maker not making ice, ice maker broken
symptoms.symptom_description: Refrigerator ice maker is not producing ice or has stopped working
symptoms.repair_guidance: Start by checking if the ice maker is turned on and the freezer temperature is set below 10°F. If water supply is confirmed, the ice maker assembly (PS11752899) is the most common cause. If the unit powers on but makes no ice, check the water inlet valve (PS11724432) — it may not be opening to let water in. If ice was being made but the unit thinks the bin is full when it is not, replace the ice level control board (PS11742379).

symptoms.symptom_keywords: not cooling, warm fridge, not cold, temperature too high, refrigerator warm, food spoiling
symptoms.symptom_description: Refrigerator is not maintaining cold temperature or food is spoiling
symptoms.repair_guidance: First check that the condenser coils (behind or beneath the unit) are not dirty — vacuum them. Then test the evaporator fan motor (PS11768502) — if it is not running, cold air cannot circulate from the freezer. A failed defrost thermostat (PS11753382) can cause ice build-up on the evaporator that blocks airflow. The compressor start relay (PS11757835) is a $19 fix worth trying before replacing the compressor itself.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: leaking water, water on floor, water pooling under fridge, dripping, water leak
symptoms.symptom_description: Water is leaking from the refrigerator onto the floor
symptoms.repair_guidance: Water leaks usually come from three places: the water inlet valve (PS11724432) if it has a cracked fitting, the ice maker assembly (PS11752899) if the fill cup is misaligned, or a damaged door gasket (PS11744531) allowing warm air condensation to drip. Check whether water appears near the back of the unit (inlet valve) or in the freezer floor (ice maker).

symptoms.symptom_keywords: loud noise, humming, buzzing, rattling, grinding, noisy fridge, clicking
symptoms.symptom_description: Refrigerator making loud or unusual noises
symptoms.repair_guidance: A loud humming or rattling often comes from the evaporator fan motor (PS11768502) — worn bearings cause a grinding noise that gets louder when you open the freezer. If the sound comes from the back bottom of the unit, suspect the condenser fan motor (PS11752316). A clicking sound every 2–3 minutes with no cooling usually means the compressor start relay (PS11757835) is failing.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: door not sealing, door gasket torn, warm door edge, condensation on door, door not closing properly
symptoms.symptom_description: Refrigerator door is not sealing properly, allowing warm air to enter
symptoms.repair_guidance: Test the seal by closing the door on a piece of paper — if it slides out easily, the gasket needs replacement. Check the fresh food door gasket (PS11744531) first, then the freezer door gasket (PS11752400). Also test the opposite door gasket (PS11744889) if you see frost at the door edge. Ensure the door hinges are tight and the door is level before assuming the gasket is the cause.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: ice maker leaking, ice maker dripping, water in ice bin, water pooling in freezer, ice clumping
symptoms.symptom_description: Ice maker is leaking water into the freezer or into the ice bin
symptoms.repair_guidance: Water leaking into the ice bin or pooling on the freezer floor usually means the water inlet valve (PS11724432) is not closing completely after a fill cycle. Ice forming in a ring around the fill tube or a freeze-up at the inlet suggests the ice maker water fill tube (PS11752611) is cracked or has shifted out of position.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: not draining, standing water, water at bottom, won't drain, water left in dishwasher
symptoms.symptom_description: Dishwasher has standing water at the bottom of the tub after a cycle completes
symptoms.repair_guidance: First clean the filter assembly (PS11743462) — a clogged filter is the number-one cause of poor draining and requires no tools, just a quarter-turn removal and rinse. If the filter is clean, the drain pump (PS11744150) is likely failing — you may hear it try to run but not move water. Also check that the drain hose is not kinked and that the garbage disposal knockout plug has been removed if you recently installed a disposal.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: not cleaning, dirty dishes, food residue, poor wash, dishes not clean, spots on dishes
symptoms.symptom_description: Dishwasher is not cleaning dishes properly — food residue remains after a full cycle
symptoms.repair_guidance: Check the filter (PS11743462) first — a dirty filter is the most common reason dishes come out with food still on them, and cleaning it is free. Then inspect the spray arms (PS11745299): hold them up to the light and check that each hole is clear — use a toothpick to clear any clogged holes. If water pressure still seems weak after cleaning, the circulation pump motor (PS11752722) may be failing and will need replacement.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: leaking, water on floor, dishwasher leak, water under dishwasher, leaking from door
symptoms.symptom_description: Dishwasher is leaking water onto the kitchen floor
symptoms.repair_guidance: Door leaks during a wash cycle almost always mean the door seal/gasket (PS11757106) has hardened or torn — inspect it for cracks or gaps. Leaks from the front lower area of the door during the wash cycle often come from the detergent dispenser (PS11731570) cracking over time. A slow drip from under the unit at the back indicates the water inlet valve (PS11748172) has a loose fitting or a hairline crack.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: door not latching, door won't close, door stays open, door falls open, can't start dishwasher
symptoms.symptom_description: Dishwasher door is not latching shut, preventing the cycle from starting
symptoms.repair_guidance: If the door does not click closed, the door latch (PS11746129) is the primary suspect — it is a quick repair with just a screwdriver and the most common cause of a dishwasher that will not start. If the door closes but swings open or feels floppy, the door balance link kit (PS11749051) or door spring (PS11751180) has likely snapped. Inspect both sides of the door at the bottom hinge area for broken straps or springs.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: not drying, wet dishes, dishes still wet after cycle, poor drying, dishes dripping
symptoms.symptom_description: Dishwasher is not drying dishes effectively — dishes come out wet after the cycle ends
symptoms.repair_guidance: First check that you are using rinse aid — it dramatically improves drying and is often overlooked. Then test the heating element (PS11751043) for continuity with a multimeter — a broken element is the most common cause of drying failure. The high limit thermostat (PS11750035) can trip and cut power to the heating element as a safety measure; test it too before assuming the element is bad. Finally, verify the vent assembly (PS11769028) opens at the end of the cycle to exhaust moist air.

symptoms.symptom_keywords: error E1, E1 error code, F8E1, water not filling, dishwasher won't fill, no water
symptoms.symptom_description: Dishwasher showing E1 error code or not filling with water at the start of a cycle
symptoms.repair_guidance: An E1 error means the dishwasher is not detecting enough water entering the tub. First check that the water supply valve under the sink is fully open. Then test the water inlet valve (PS11748172) — a failed solenoid will not open to let water in. Also check the float switch (PS11751280): locate the small float dome in the front corner of the tub and press it down — if it is stuck in the up position, it will signal the tub is full even when it is empty.
